title: Surface area
description: Practice finding the surface area of 3D objects.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the surface area of the cube shown below.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units$^2$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Let's draw a net of the cube.


[[☃ image 1]]

All  $6$ of the faces are squares.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= \dfrac23 \cdot \dfrac23\\\\
&= \dfrac{2 \cdot2}{3 \cdot 3} \\\\
&= \blueD{\dfrac{4}{9}}\\\\
\end{align}$

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area of a cube} &=  6 \cdot \blueD{\text{Area of a square}}\\\\
&= 6 \cdot \blueD{\dfrac{4}{9}}\\\\
&= \dfrac{6 \cdot4}{ 9} \\\\
&= \dfrac{\stackrel{2}{\cancel{6}}\cdot4 }{\underset{3}{\cancel9}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{2 \cdot 4}{3}\\\\
&= \dfrac{8}{3}
\end{align}$
The surface area of the cube is $\dfrac{8}{3}$ units$^2$.

options.alt: A cube. The height is 2 thirds unit.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the surface area of the cube shown below.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units$^2$


[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Let's draw a net of the cube.

[[☃ image 1]]

All  $6$ of the faces are squares.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 2 \dfrac12 \cdot 2 \dfrac12\\\\
&= \dfrac52 \cdot \dfrac52\\\\
&= \dfrac{5 \cdot5}{2 \cdot 2} \\\\
&= \blueD{\dfrac{25}{4}}\\\\
\end{align}$

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area of a cube} &=  6 \cdot \blueD{\text{Area of a square}}\\\\
&= 6 \cdot \blueD{\dfrac{25}{4}}\\\\
&= \dfrac{6 \cdot25}{ 4} \\\\
&= \dfrac{\stackrel{3}{\cancel{6}}\cdot25 }{\underset{2}{\cancel4}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{3 \cdot 25}{2}\\\\
&= \dfrac{75}{2}
\end{align}$
The surface area of the cube is $\dfrac{75}{2}$ units$^2$.

options.alt: A cube. The height is 2 and 1 half units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The present shown below is a cube.

[[☃ image 4]]

**Find the surface area of the present.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ cm}^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the present.

[[☃ image 1]]

All  $6$ of the faces are squares.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 20 \cdot 20\\\\
&= \blueE{400}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area of a cube} &=  6 \cdot \blueE{\text{Area of a square}}\\\\
&= 6 \cdot \blueE{400}\\\\
&= 2{,}400
\end{align}$
The surface area of the present is $2{,}400 \text{ cm}^2$.

options.alt: A gift that is a cube. The side lengths are 20 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The present shown below is a cube.

[[☃ image 4]]

**Find the surface area of the present.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ cm}^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the present.


[[☃ image 1]]

All  $6$ of the faces are squares.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 15 \cdot 15\\\\
&= \blueE{225}
\end{align}$

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area of a cube} &=  6 \cdot \blueE{\text{Area of a square}}\\\\
&= 6 \cdot \blueE{225}\\\\
&= 1{,}350
\end{align}$
The surface area of the present is $1{,}350 \text{ cm}^2$.

options.alt: A gift that is a cube. The side lengths are 15 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the surface area of the right rectangular prism shown below.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units$^2$



[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Let's draw a net of the rectangular prism.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each of the sides is a $4$ by $2$ rectangle. 

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 4 \cdot 2\\\\
&= {8} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the sides  is $2 \cdot 8 = \blueD{16}$.

The bottom and the top are both $4$ by $2.5$ rectangles.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 4 \cdot 2.5\\\\
&= {10} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the bottom and the top is $2 \cdot 10 = \greenD{20}$.

The back and the front are both $2$ by $2.5$ rectangles.


>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 2 \cdot 2.5\\\\
&= {5} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the back and the front  is $2 \cdot 5 = \goldD{10}$.

Let's add the areas to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{16}+ \greenD{20} + \goldD{10} \\\\
&= 46\\\\
\end{align}$
The surface area of the rectangular prism is $46$ units$^2$.

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The length is 2.5 units. The width is 4 units. The height is 2 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the surface area of the right rectangular prism shown below.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units$^2$


[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Let's draw a net of the rectangular prism.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each of the sides is a $4$ by $1.5$ rectangle. 

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 4 \cdot 1.5\\\\
&= {6} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the sides  is $2 \cdot 6 = \blueD{12}$.

The bottom and the top are both $4$ by $5$ rectangles.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 4 \cdot 5\\\\
&= {20} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of bottom and the top is $2 \cdot 20 = \greenD{40}$.

The back and the front are both $1.5$ by $5$ rectangles.


>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 1.5 \cdot 5\\\\
&= {7.5} \\\\
\end{align}$

The total area of the back and the front  is $2 \cdot 7.5 = \goldD{15}$.

Let's add the areas to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{12}+ \greenD{40} + \goldD{15} \\\\
&= 67\\\\
\end{align}$
The surface area of the rectangular prism is $67$ units$^2$.

options.alt: A rectangular prism. The length is 5 units. The width is 4 units. The height is 1.5 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The cereal box shown below is a rectangular prism.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Find the surface area of the cereal box.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ cm}^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the cereal box.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each of the sides is a $5$ by $30$ rectangle. 

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 5 \cdot 30\\\\
&= {150}
\end{align}$

The total area of the sides  is $2 \cdot 150 = \blueE{300}$.

The bottom and the top are both $5$ by $20$ rectangles.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 5 \cdot 20\\\\
&= {100}
\end{align}$

The total area of the bottom and the top is $2 \cdot 100 = \purpleD{200}$.

The back and the front are both $30$ by $20$ rectangles.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 30 \cdot 20\\\\
&= {600}
\end{align}$

The total area of the back and the front  is $2 \cdot 600 = \goldE{1{,}200}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueE{300}+ \purpleD{200} + \goldE{1{,}200} \\\\
&= 1{,}700
\end{align}$
The surface area of the cereal box is $1{,}700\text{ cm}^2$.

options.alt: A cereal box that is a rectangular prism. The length is 20 centimeters. The width is 5 centimeters. The height is 30 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The cereal box shown below is a rectangular prism.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Find the surface area of the cereal box.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ cm}^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the cereal box.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each of the sides is a $4$ by $25$ rectangle. 

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 4 \cdot 25\\\\
&= {100}
\end{align}$

The total area of the sides  is $2 \cdot 100 = \blueE{200}$.

The bottom and the top are both $4$ by $15$ rectangles.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 4 \cdot 15\\\\
&= {60}
\end{align}$

The total area of the bottom and the top is $2 \cdot 60 = \purpleD{120}$.

The back and the front are both $25$ by $15$ rectangles.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 25 \cdot 15\\\\
&= {375}
\end{align}$

The total area of the back and the front  is $2 \cdot 375 = \goldE{750}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueE{200}+ \purpleD{120} + \goldE{750} \\\\
&= 1{,}070
\end{align}$
The surface area of the cereal box is $1{,}070\text{ cm}^2$.

options.alt: A cereal box that is a rectangular prism. The length is 15 centimeters. The width is 4 centimeters. The height is 25 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
The cereal box shown below is a rectangular prism.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Find the surface area of the cereal box.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ cm}^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the cereal box.

[[☃ image 1]]

Each of the sides is a $3$ by $20$ rectangle. 

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 3 \cdot 20\\\\
&= {60}
\end{align}$

The total area of the sides  is $2 \cdot 60 = \blueE{120}$.

The bottom and the top are both $3$ by $10$ rectangles.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 3 \cdot 10\\\\
&= {30}
\end{align}$

The total area of the bottom and the top is $2 \cdot 30 = \purpleD{60}$.

The back and the front are both $20$ by $10$ rectangles.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 20 \cdot 10\\\\
&= {200}
\end{align}$

The total area of the back and the front  is $2 \cdot 200 = \goldE{400}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueE{120}+ \purpleE{60} + \goldE{400} \\\\
&= 580
\end{align}$
The surface area of the cereal box is $580\text{ cm}^2$.

options.alt: A cereal box that is a rectangular prism. The length is 10 centimeters. The width is 3 centimeters. The height is 20 centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the surface area of the right triangular prism shown below.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units$^2$


[[☃ image 1]]
hints:
Let's draw a net of the triangular prism.

[[☃ image 1]]

$1$ of the faces is an $11$ by $3$ rectangle.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 11 \cdot 3\\\\
&= {\blueD{33}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$1$ of the faces is an $11$ by $4$ rectangle.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 11 \cdot 4\\\\
&= {\greenD{44}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$1$ of the faces is an $11$ by $5$ rectangle.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 11 \cdot 5\\\\
&= {\goldD{55}} \\\\
\end{align}$

$2$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

>$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 4 \cdot 3\\\\
&= 6 \\\\
\end{align}$


The total area of these $2$ triangles is $2 \cdot 6 = \maroonD{12}$.

Let's add the areas to find the surface area.

>$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueD{33}+ \greenD{44} + \goldD{55} + \maroonD{12} \\\\
&= 144\\\\
\end{align}$
The surface area of the triangular prism is $144$ units$^2$.

options.alt: A right triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 4 units and 11 units. The side face has side lengths of 3 units and 11 units. The diagonal side face has side lengths of 5 units and 11 units. The triangular prism has a height of 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the surface area of the triangular prism shown below.**

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units$^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the triangular prism.


[[☃ image 1]]

$2$ of the faces are $7$ by $5$ rectangles. 

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 7 \cdot 5\\\\
&= {35}
\end{align}$

The total area of these $2$ rectangles  is $2 \cdot 35 = \maroonD{70}$.

$1$ of the faces is a $7$ by $6$ rectangle.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 7 \cdot 6\\\\
&= \purpleD{42}
\end{align}$

$2$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 6 \cdot 4\\\\
&= 12
\end{align}$

The total area of these $2$ triangles is $2 \cdot 12 = \goldE{24}$.

Let's add the areas to find the surface area.

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \maroonD{70}+ \purpleD{42} + \goldE{24} \\\\
&= 136
\end{align}$
The surface area of the triangular prism is $136$ units$^2$.

options.alt: A triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 6 units and 7 units. The other 2 faces have side lengths of 5 units and 7 units. The triangular prism has a height of 4 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the surface area of the triangular prism shown below.**

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units$^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the triangular prism.


[[☃ image 1]]

$2$ of the faces are $14$ by $10$ rectangles. 

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 14 \cdot 10\\\\
&= {140}
\end{align}$

The total area of these $2$ rectangles  is $2 \cdot 140 = \maroonD{280}$.

$1$ of the faces is a $14$ by $12$ rectangle.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 14 \cdot 12\\\\
&= \purpleD{168}
\end{align}$

$2$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 12 \cdot 8\\\\
&= 48
\end{align}$

The total area of these $2$ triangles is $2 \cdot 48 = \goldE{96}$.

Let's add the areas to find the surface area.

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \maroonD{280}+ \purpleD{168} + \goldE{96} \\\\
&= 544
\end{align}$
The surface area of the triangular prism is $544$ units$^2$.

options.alt: A triangular prism. The bottom face has side lengths of 12 units and 14 units. The other 2 faces have side lengths of 10 units and 14 units. The triangular prism has a height of 8 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Cam's tent (shown below) is a triangular prism.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Find the surface area, including the floor, of his tent.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ m}^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the tent.



[[☃ image 1]]

$3$ of the faces are $3$ by $2$ rectangles. 

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 3 \cdot 2\\\\
&= {6}
\end{align}$

The total area of these $3$ rectangles  is $3 \cdot 6 = \blueE{18}$.

$2$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 2 \cdot 1.7\\\\
&= 1.7
\end{align}$

The total area of these $2$ triangles is $2 \cdot 1.7 = \goldE{3.4}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueE{18}+  \goldE{3.4} \\\\
&= 21.4
\end{align}$
The surface area of the tent is $21.4\text{ m}^2$.

options.alt: A triangular prism. A dashed line that is perpendicular to the base of the triangular face starts at the middle of the base and goes straight up to the top vertex to the triangle. This dashed line represents the height and is 1.7 meters. An arrow starts at the bottom, left vertex of the triangular face and ends at the dashed line. This length is 1 meter. An arrow starts at dashed line and ends at the bottom, right vertex of the triangular face. This length is 1 meter. The other 2 lengths of the triangular face are 2 meters. All 3 rectangular faces of the prism have side lengths of 2 meters and 3 meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Shaun's tent (shown below) is a triangular prism.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Find the surface area, including the floor, of his tent.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ m}^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the tent.



[[☃ image 1]]

$3$ of the faces are $5$ by $3$ rectangles. 

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a rectangle} &=  \text{length} \cdot \text{width}\\\\
&= 5 \cdot 3\\\\
&= {15}
\end{align}$

The total area of these $3$ rectangles  is $3 \cdot 15 = \blueE{45}$.

$2$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 3 \cdot 2.6\\\\
&= 3.9
\end{align}$

The total area of these $2$ triangles is $2 \cdot 3.9 = \goldE{7.8}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueE{45}+  \goldE{7.8} \\\\
&= 52.8
\end{align}$
The surface area of the tent is $52.8\text{ m}^2$.

options.alt: A triangular prism. A dashed line that is perpendicular to the base of the triangular face starts at the middle of the base and goes straight up to the top vertex to the triangle. This dashed line represents the height and is 2.6 meters. An arrow starts at the bottom, left vertex of the triangular face and ends at the dashed line. This length is 1.5 meters. An arrow starts at dashed line and ends at the bottom, right vertex of the triangular face. This length is 1.5 meters. The other 2 lengths of the triangular face are 3 meters. All 3 rectangular faces of the prism have side lengths of 3 meters and 5 meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the surface area of the square pyramid shown below.**

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units$^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the square pyramid.



[[☃ image 1]]

$1$ of the faces is a square.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 4 \cdot 4\\\\
&= {\maroonD{16}}
\end{align}$

$4$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 4 \cdot 3\\\\
&= 6
\end{align}$

The total area of these $4$ triangles is $4 \cdot 6 = \purpleD{24}$.

Let's add the areas to find the surface area.

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \maroonD{16}+ \purpleD{24}\\\\
&= 40
\end{align}$
The surface area of the square pyramid is $40$ units$^2$.

options.alt: A square pyramid.  The side of the pyramid's base is 4 units long and the height of the triangular face measured from the center of the side of base to the apex is 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Find the surface area of the square pyramid shown below.**

[[☃ image 2]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] units$^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the square pyramid.


[[☃ image 1]]

$1$ of the faces is a square.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 8 \cdot 8\\\\
&= {\maroonD{64}}
\end{align}$

$4$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 8 \cdot 6\\\\
&= 24
\end{align}$

The total area of these $4$ triangles is $4 \cdot 24 = \purpleD{96}$.

Let's add the areas to find the surface area.

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \maroonD{64}+ \purpleD{96}\\\\
&= 160
\end{align}$
The surface area of the square pyramid is $160$ units$^2$.

options.alt: A square pyramid.  The side of the pyramid's base is 8 units long and the height of the triangular face measured from the center of the side of base to the apex is 6 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ben made a model (shown below) of the square pyramid he plans to build when he grows up.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Find the surface area of the model.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ m}^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the square pyramid.



[[☃ image 1]]

$1$ of the faces is a square.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 10 \cdot 10\\\\
&= \blueE{100}
\end{align}$

$4$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 10 \cdot 7\\\\
&= 35
\end{align}$

The total area of these $4$ triangles is $4 \cdot 35 = \purpleD{140}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueE{100}+ \purpleD{140}\\\\
&= 240
\end{align}$
The surface area of the square pyramid is $240\text{ m}^2$.

options.alt: A square pyramid.  The side of the pyramid's base is 10 units long and the height of the triangular face measured from the center of the side of base to the apex is 7 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Marie made a model (shown below) of the square pyramid she plans to build when she grows up. 

[[☃ image 2]]

**Find the surface area of the model.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ m}^2$
hints:
Let's draw a net of the square pyramid.



[[☃ image 1]]

$1$ of the faces is a square.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 12 \cdot 12\\\\
&= \blueE{144}
\end{align}$

$4$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 12 \cdot 8\\\\
&= 48
\end{align}$

The total area of these $4$ triangles is $4 \cdot 48 = \purpleD{192}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.

$\begin{align} \text{Surface area} &= \blueE{144}+ \purpleD{192}\\\\
&= 336
\end{align}$
The surface area of the square pyramid is $336\text{ m}^2$.

options.alt: A square pyramid.  The side of the pyramid's base is 12 units long and the height of the triangular face measured from the center of the side of base to the apex is 8 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Marie made a model (shown below) of the square pyramid she plans to build when she grows up.

[[☃ image 2]]

**Find the surface area of the model.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ m}^2$
hints:

[[☃ image 1]]

$1$ of the faces is a square.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a square} &=  \text{side} \cdot \text{side}\\\\
&= 8 \cdot 8\\\\
&= {\blueE{64}}
\end{align}$

$4$ of the faces are triangles. Each triangle has the same base and height.

$\begin{align} \text{Area of a triangle} &=  \dfrac12 \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac12 \cdot 8 \cdot 5\\\\
&= 20
\end{align}$

The total area of these $4$ triangles is $4 \cdot 20 = \purpleD{80}$.

Let's add the areas we found to find the surface area.
